completed_tasks.name: Init phase 19, scout codebase, load prior context (PROJECT.md, REQUIREMENTS.md, STATE.md, CONTEXT.md from phases 14/15/17, existing skills code in core/tools)
Identify 7 gray areas for Phase 19 (conditional activation, env/credential UX, settings namespace, security scanning, Skills Hub, credential mounting, hermes metadata extraction strategy)
Present gray areas to user with code-context annotations and prior-decision carry-forward

remaining_tasks.name: User selects which gray areas to discuss
Discuss each selected area (4 questions per area, with code/prior-context annotations)

blockers.description: Context window pressure (~81%) on the paused session — fresh session recommended for the question/answer loop
blockers.workaround: Resume in fresh session; re-read the listed prior-context files plus this handoff.

human_actions_pending.action: Pick which of the 7 gray areas to discuss for Phase 19 (or reply 'all', or 'skip 5' if Skills Hub should split off into its own phase)
human_actions_pending.context: Skills Hub alone is ~2,691 LOC in Python reference (skills_hub.py); largest scope item by far.

next_action: Re-read .planning/phases/19-skills-framework/.continue-here.md and the 7 gray areas listed; ask user which to discuss; then resume discuss-phase workflow at the discuss_areas step.
context_notes: Phase 19 Skills Framework adds the v2.0 layers on top of the existing Phase 7/7.1/7.2 baseline (SkillRegistry, SkillsTool, SkillsConfig, platform filter, opaque metadata). REQUIREMENTS SKILL-01..SKILL-11 cover SKILL.md format, conditional activation, env vars/credentials, security scanning, settings namespace, sandbox pass-through, and Skills Hub with trust levels. SKILL-12/13/14 (slash commands) are Phase 20. The biggest open question is whether Skills Hub stays in Phase 19 or splits off given its size (~2,691 LOC of Python reference).
